concentration.headline: Primate city dominance — one city overwhelms all others
concentration.body: Dar es Salaam accounts for a striking 39.9% of tracked urban population across 90 cities in Tanzania. This level of primacy (40%) is characteristic of rapidly urbanising or historically centralised economies where one city concentrates political, economic and cultural power.

gravity.headline: Dense gravitational cluster — Dar es Salaam & Ubungo
gravity.body: The strongest gravitational pull exists between Dar es Salaam and Ubungo (9 km apart, combined population 9,048,912). Cities form a tight gravitational cluster with top pairs averaging 9 km apart. This dense sub-regional network often functions as a single megalopolitan economy.

narration.synthesis: Tanzania exhibits strong primate city dominance: Dar es Salaam holds 39.9% of tracked urban population — a striking concentration. The gravity network confirms this: connections involving Dar es Salaam dominate, with Dar es Salaam → Ubungo among secondary links. This pattern is characteristic of historically centralised or rapidly urbanising economies.
